prose:
Computed property: frontier-orbital energies and HOMO–LUMO gap of formaldehyde

Method provenance
• Property: HOMO, LUMO, and HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans-style orbital energies) in electron-volts (eV).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier "standard" = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user (file “formaldehyde.xyz”); no re-optimization was performed (single-point calculation → DFT//exp-geom).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill (PySCF v2 backend; invoked via chemkit_engine CLI, see command below).
• Convergence & warnings: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine.  Integrity check status: ok, trustworthy = true.

Key results (electronic energies):
  HOMO  = −7.6523067 eV
  LUMO  = −1.7826355 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV  ← headline value

Reproducibility
The job was launched with:
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptgk3t828/live.json
A live engine log was written during execution to the automatically generated file “frontier-orbitals_<timestamp>.out” in the working directory; it can be inspected with e.g. `tail -f` for full SCF details. The summarized JSON result is stored at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptgk3t828/live.json.

No warnings were reported by the tool.

Method used: chemkit “frontier-orbitals” skill with DFT (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, tier standard) in the gas phase.
